goals: Keep eMuleBB support as a first-class fork feature.
Keep generic aMuTorrent behavior as close to upstream as possible.
Make upstream rebases conflict-driven and test-driven instead of memory-driven.

buildTooling.browserslistPolicy: Tailwind 3 bundles Browserslist and caniuse-lite data, so root CSS builds suppress its stale-data warning until a deliberate Tailwind 4 migration.

forkOwned.reason: eMuleBB REST client manager and ED2K adapter
Windows portable package setup and runtime launcher
Fork-owned contract tests for the eMuleBB manager

sharedSeams.reason: Static client type metadata and capability declarations
Runtime client-manager lookup shared by all client types
Environment and persisted client configuration mapping
First-run setup flow that exposes eMuleBB as an ED2K backend

upstreamOwned.rule: Do not edit during fork maintenance unless a user-facing feature requires it.
Prefer upstream changes unless eMuleBB release packaging needs an explicit delta.
Prefer upstream content; keep fork-specific workspace docs in eMule-tooling.

rebaseWorkflow: Resolve forkOwned paths by preserving fork contracts unless upstream has equivalent support.
Resolve sharedSeams by keeping tests green and minimizing generic-client drift.
acceptance: eMuleBB remains registered as an ED2K client type.
eMuleBB setup configuration remains addressable from environment and UI metadata.
qBittorrent compatibility APIs do not proxy through eMuleBB.
Windows package runner keeps data package-local and supports Node 24 or newer.
